Code fragment: public final TableField<BookRecord, Integer> ID = createField("id", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key is named generically 'id' instead of a more descriptive name like 'book_id'. Consider renaming the primary key to 'book_id' to make it more meaningful and easier to identify in queries and joins.

Using selectFrom(BOOK) fetches all columns from the BOOK table, which can transfer unnecessary data and reduce performance. Consider using select() with only the specific columns needed (e.g., select(BOOK.ISBN, BOOK.TITLE, ...).from(BOOK)).

Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides ('%search%') for text searching prevents index usage and performs poorly on large datasets. Consider using a dedicated full-text search solution like MySQL's FULLTEXT index with MATCH...AGAINST or an external search engine like Elasticsearch.
